criteria.criterion: The 4 crash-point subtests in TestSplitAtomicCommit call RecoverSplitCommits against freshly reconstructed catalog/tree/appender objects (e.g. via catalog.RecoverFromWAL re-application onto brand-new, freshly-opened objects) instead of the same in-memory objects ExecuteSplitAtomic partially mutated.
criteria.planned_check: crashPointTest now calls reopenFreshSplitDeps (opens new *catalog.FileManager/*catalog.Catalog + catalog.RecoverFromWAL, new *btree.Tree via openTestBtreeAt, new *graph.EdgeAppender via graph.OpenEdgeAppender) before every RecoverSplitCommits call; both RecoverSplitCommits invocations and all post-recovery assertions route through the fresh objects (freshDeps), not deps.cat/deps.tree/deps.appender.
criteria.result: PASS (all 8 subtests, including all 4 crash-point subtests: crash_before_commit_append_no_visible_effect, crash_after_commit_before_catalog_put_recovers_fully, crash_after_catalog_put_before_btree_recovers_fully, crash_after_btree_before_graph_recovers_fully)

criteria.criterion: Simulation is faithful to a real process restart (implicit acceptance criterion from subtask wording).
criteria.planned_check: Fresh *catalog.Catalog starts with an empty in-memory index (per catalog.NewCatalog's documented behavior) and is rebuilt solely via catalog.RecoverFromWAL replaying WAL records -- no reliance on any in-process index state surviving the simulated crash. Fresh *btree.Tree is backed by a brand-new NodeStore/NodeAllocator (new *os.File handle) over the same index file. Fresh *graph.EdgeAppender is a brand-new OpenEdgeAppender call. Verified by code review of reopenFreshSplitDeps plus passing test run.
criteria.test_spec: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestSplitAtomicCommit -v (manual inspection of PASS output for each crash-point subtest)

criteria.criterion: No regression in the rest of engine/split's test suite.

criteria.planned_check: go vet on the touched package.

note: This validation matrix and its results are internal self-consistency checks performed by the implementation agent per invariant I4 (no self-verification). Independent verification is deferred to /cdr:verify.
